classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: That Sin is Not the Striving for an Evil Nature, But the Desertion of a Better.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Likewise because sin, or unrighteousness, is not the striving after evil nature but the desertion of better, it is thus found written in the Scriptures: "Every creature of God is good."

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: And accordingly every tree also which God planted in Paradise is assuredly good.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Man did not therefore strive after an evil nature when he touched the forbidden tree; but by deserting what was better, he committed an evil deed.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Since the Creator is better than any creature which He has made, His command should not have been deserted, that the thing forbidden, however good, might be touched; since the better having been deserted, the good of the creature was striven for, which was touched contrary to the command of the Creator.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: God did not plant an evil tree in Paradise; but He Himself was better who prohibited its being touched.
